title: Barcode Standards
objectives: Choose the right symbology per use case

sections.markdown: GS1 defines a family of symbologies for different use cases. Each is a specification for how data becomes bars/dots — the identifier itself (GTIN, SSCC…) is separate from how it's encoded.

sections.code:
Application Identifiers (AI) — the language of GS1-128 and GS1 2D:
  01  GTIN                    (14 digits)
  10  Batch/Lot               (variable, up to 20)
  11  Production date         (YYMMDD)
  17  Expiry date             (YYMMDD)
  21  Serial number           (variable, up to 20)
  00  SSCC                    (18 digits)
  310n Net weight (kg, n = dec places)

FNC1 delimits variable-length AIs from the next AI.

sections.markdown: Two dimensions of choice: **symbology** (how bars/dots encode data) and **data content** (which AIs and identifiers you include). Choose symbology by physical constraints (small pack? DataMatrix). Choose AIs by workflow (pharma needs GTIN+batch+expiry+serial).
Using EAN-13 on a case/carton — POS won't accept it. Use ITF-14 or GS1-128 for logistics units. Conversely, printing GS1-128 on a consumer pack: retail POS may not be configured for it.

flashcards.back: Logistics labels — encodes SSCC and other AIs on cases/pallets.
A short numeric prefix (01, 10, 17, 21, …) that says what the following digits mean.

quiz.q: For a small pharma vial serialized to the unit, choose…
For a pallet with SSCC + shipment details use…

challenges.text: Given AI-encoded string, decode into GTIN/batch/expiry fields.
Encode SSCC + weight for a GS1-128 label.
Write a symbology chooser for use cases: (retail unit, case, pallet, pharma).
Build an end-to-end labeler that picks symbology + AIs from a product spec.

interviewQuestions: How do you choose a symbology?
When would you pick DataMatrix over QR?
